title: The Machine Is Working: Opendoor Pushing for Profitability by End of 2026
text: Opendoor's executives remain optimistic about achieving profitability by year-end, despite mixed Q1 2026 results. The company reduced fixed operating expenses to $33 million in Q1, down from $35 million the previous quarter. Acquisition contracts reached their highest level since 2022, doubling from Q4 to over 5,000. Additionally, homes listed over 120 days declined dramatically from 33% to 10%. Revenue decreased to $720 million in Q1 from $736 million in Q4, yet gross profit improved to $72 million. Adjusted EBITDA losses narrowed from $43 million to $31 million. CEO Kaz Nejatian stated the company's machine is working, emphasizing that Opendoor operates on a different model than the legacy framework. He noted that as of April 1, adjusted EBITDA turned profitable on a forward 12-month basis. CFO Christy Schwartz highlighted the significance of recovery, saying the company has gone from its lowest contract volume since COVID to its highest since 2022. For Q2, Opendoor projects 25% quarter-over-quarter revenue growth and expects adjusted EBITDA to reach break-even. The company continues expanding AI integration across underwriting and other operations.
